chapter_title.en: Chapter on Those Permitted to Depart Early From Muzdalifah Before Fajr

text: 2993 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تُقَدَّمَ اَلنِّسَاءُ إِذَا زَالَ اَللَّيْلُ فَيَقِفْنَ عِنْدَ اَلْمَشْعَرِ اَلْحَرَامِ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ يُنْطَلَقُ بِهِنَّ إِلَى مِنًى فَيَرْمِينَ اَلْجَمْرَةَ ثُمَّ يَصْبِرْنَ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ يُقَصِّرْنَ وَ يُنْطَلَقُ بِهِنَّ إِلَى مَكَّةَ فَيَطُفْنَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُنَّ يُرِدْنَ أَنْ يُذْبَحَ عَنْهُنَّ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يُوَكِّلْنَ مَنْ يَذْبَحُ عَنْهُنَّ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2993 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
"There is no harm in allowing women to depart earlier when the night has passed. They should stand at Al-Mash'ar Al-Haram for a brief moment, then proceed to Mina to throw the pebbles at the Jamarah. After that, they should wait briefly, then shorten their hair, and proceed to Mecca to perform tawaf. However, if they wish to have the sacrifice performed on their behalf, they should appoint someone to perform it for them."
